executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 98 organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours, spanning critical sectors including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital), education (Eastern Townships School Board), public sector (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health), and major brands (Canada Goose hit twice). The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 18 victims, followed by CLOP with 15 and Play with 10. Additionally, the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an updated alert for three critical vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD network security devices that require immediate patching. The volume and breadth of these attacks represent a national crisis requiring urgent coordinated response.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a ransomware crisis with 98 organizations victimized in 48 hours across all sectors. CCCS has issued Alert AL25-012 (Update 1) warning of three vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD devices requiring urgent patching. Critical infrastructure including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Thunder Bay Counselling), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collège Supérieur De Montreal, Teccart College), and municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health) have been compromised.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose has been hit by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross automotive retail chain and Kids & Company childcare services were also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers, multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, Bergmanis Preyra law firm with financial practice), and financial services organizations were also hit. No major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) identified in this reporting period.

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has added eight actively exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including three critical Cisco Catalyst switch flaws (CVE-2026-20122, CVE-2026-20128, CVE-2026-20133) with a 9.0 CVSS score that allow attackers to compromise entire networks via a single file upload. A self-propagating npm supply chain attack is actively spreading, stealing developer authentication tokens through compromised packages. Canada Life insurance suffered a data breach exposing up to 70,000 customer records through a Salesforce incident, and Iranian-linked threat actors (313 Team, state-sponsored groups) are actively conducting DDoS attacks against Bluesky and AI-powered executive impersonation campaigns targeting financial institutions. The Lazarus Group executed a $290 million heist against KelpDAO using compromised RPC nodes and DDoS tactics.

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korean state-sponsored)
items.targets: KelpDAO cryptocurrency platform ($290M theft), businesses via ClickFix attacks, macOS users
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1189 (Drive-by Compromise), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), Compromised RPC nodes, DDoS attacks on healthy infrastructure, AppleScript-based credential stealers

items.name: 313 Team (Iran-linked)
items.targets: Bluesky social media platform, UK organizations
items.ttps: T1498 (Network Denial of Service), DDoS attacks lasting approximately 24 hours

items.name: Iranian state-sponsored actors
items.targets: Financial institutions, VIP users, UK organizations, Albania data exfiltration
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), AI-powered executive impersonation, targeting trust relationships in workflows

items.name: Scattered Spider (aka Tylerb)
items.targets: Multiple victims via smishing and SIM swap attacks totaling approximately $8M
items.ttps: T1566.003 (Phishing: Spearphishing via Service), T1528 (Steal Application Access Token), SMS-based phishing, SIM swapping attacks

items.name: BlackCat Ransomware Group (ALPHV)
items.targets: Multiple organizations globally
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), Ransomware operations with negotiator assistance

items.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware
items.targets: 1,570+ victims revealed via SystemBC C2 server analysis
items.ttps: T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), SystemBC proxy malware for C2 communications

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), Double extortion tactics

canada_landscape.summary: Canada Life insurance company suffered a data breach through a Salesforce incident, exposing personally identifiable information for up to 70,000 customers—predominantly Canadian policyholders. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. The breach underscores vendor security risks in the Canadian financial services sector.
canada_landscape.financial: Canada Life insurance company: data breach via Salesforce exposing up to 70,000 customer records with PII. The breach affected mostly Canadian customers with reports indicating 5.6 million records were involved in the larger incident.
